comments: Repeat of the Yes concert broadcast on the very first FRS.

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1. And I'm Tommy Vance. Welcome to this very special edition of the Friday Rock Show.
You missed the whole program, didn't you? Well, tonight we devote the whole program to a rebroadcast of our first show of the series. As recorded at Wembley last October, yes, in concert.
And we've had loads of requests to hear this event again.
And just to mention a few names, Mark Rawsthorne of Bury in Lancashire,
Mr. Rastus Watermelon, how you doing, of Ashford in Kent,
Tim Diamond of Oldham in Lancashire,
a gentleman by the name of Peter Blenchmore of somewhere in the United Kingdom,
and Ellis Fagg of Maidstone in Kent.
Just a few of the names who are now going to sit back along with you
and enjoy Yes! in Concert.
The circus of heaven came to town.
go don't kill the wind and that was yes in concert great to hear it again the recording
engineer at wembley was neil burn and sound mix was by bill aitken and nigel luby production was
by tony wilson look out for an interview with john anderson in new york soon to be heard in rock on
definitely in tomorrow's rock on you can hear the ruts and a chap with a living legend john
male that's at four o'clock tomorrow afternoon on radio one also tomorrow at 6 30 you can hear
Roxy Music as recorded in concert
at the Hammersmith Odeon. And on Sunday,
Star Special at 7 o'clock features
Justin Haywood of the Moody's, playing
two hours of his choice of music.
Next week, our classic session
on the Friday Rock Show is by Deep Purple.
See you tomorrow at 4 for Rock On.
Till then, take care of yourselves
and have a great night.
